front: What is the primary purpose of a JTextField in Swing?
back: JTextField is a lightweight component that allows the editing of a single line of text. It is commonly used to get input from a user.

front: Which commonly used constructor creates a JTextField with a specified number of columns?
back: JTextField(int columns). This constructor creates an empty TextField with the specified number of columns, which is used to calculate the preferred width.

front: What type of event is typically listened for in a JTextField to perform an action after text entry (e.g., pressing Enter)?
back: An ActionEvent. You add an ActionListener to the JTextField to perform an action when the user types the Enter key.

front: Name two important methods for getting and setting the text contained in a JTextField.
back: getText(): Returns the String text contained in the field. setText(String text): Sets the text of the field to the specified string.

front: How can you make a JTextField read-only or non-editable for the user?
back: By using the setEditable(false) method. This allows the text to be selected and copied by the user but not modified.
